oneLiner: The protein drink that looks and feels like a treat: light, refreshing, and always in-style for your everyday moments.
positioningStatement: PEP is the protein drink for modern, wellness-focused individuals who want to enjoy healthy choices as effortless lifestyle upgrades. It delivers the feel-good refreshment of a treat while supporting daily balance, never sacrificing taste or style.
audience: Wellness enthusiasts who value self-image and healthy habits
Urban professionals and creatives in social or shared spaces (cafés, co-working, beach)
People who want their beverage to signal taste, balance, and aspiration—not just function
occasions: Casual social hangs (without the gym or party pressure)

voice.tone: Friendly, modern, softly upbeat, confidently healthy. Warm—not clinical. Aspirational, but never elitist.

visual.mood: Light, inviting, and modern. Feels like a treat in a sunlit café or stylish brunch spot.
visual.photography: Natural light, candid lifestyle shots—moments of real relaxation and sociability in attractive, aspirational but accessible settings. No sterile backgrounds or gym scenes.
visual.typography: Primary: Didot (logo), with Montserrat for clean, modern body and badge text. Elegant script touch from Brittany Signature for taglines and friendly moments.

inLineBrands.name: BLOOM
inLineBrands.why: Integrates prebiotic benefits with fun lifestyle branding, appealing to wellness-oriented, social consumers. Treats health as identity, not restriction.

inLineBrands.name: EVER TREE
inLineBrands.why: Minimal, natural, elegant—helps consumers upgrade their daily rituals with class and subtle wellness.

inLineBrands.name: OLIPOP
inLineBrands.why: Soft, friendly, approachable wellness—appeals to health-seekers outside the hardcore/fad zone.

inLineBrands.name: Poppi
inLineBrands.why: Vibrant, lively, and rooted in wellness culture; places flavor and fun on par with health.

inLineBrands.name: Alive & Well
inLineBrands.why: Smooth, approachable kombucha promising wellness without extreme flavors—feels artisanal yet very accessible.

inLineBrands.name: Recess
inLineBrands.why: Pastel, minimal, and playfully calming. Designed for relaxed, lifestyle moments—very close in occasion and vibe.

inLineBrands.name: Ole cocktails
inLineBrands.why: Vibrant, accessible, and modern—makes the act of drinking feel like an everyday upgrade. Festival vibes without being garish.

inLineBrands.name: Ish
inLineBrands.why: Sophisticated, modern, and aspirational, with a wellness and premium lifestyle orientation.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: HOLY
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Too function/benefit driven—focuses on vitamins and health optimization rather than lifestyle or identity.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: LMNT
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Sports nutrition and hydration focus; design is clinical and lacks lifestyle or aspirational cues.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: NOCCO
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: BCAA/energy positioning, gym-centric. Too functional and routine-based for PEP's lifestyle upgrade audience.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: PROTOCHOL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Protein + alcohol concept is more 'fitness with a twist.' Not fitting the wellness lifestyle or everyday treat context.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: VITAMIN WELL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Functional hydration, more about performance and health markers than identity or enjoyment.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Kin Euphorics
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Very social-focused, designed around social night and mood enhancement rather than day-to-day lifestyle upgrades.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Lyre's
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Non-alcoholic spirits; relevant for social ritual but too specifically a spirits replacement, not a general lifestyle option.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Seedlip
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Premium non-alcoholic spirit, mostly for social night and cocktail occasions—less everyday and refreshing than PEP.

antiReferences.name: Lean Body / gym RTD
antiReferences.why: Too fitness-bro; all about muscle and gym performance, opposed to PEP's inclusive, lifestyle-friendly vibe.

antiReferences.name: drinkpep.com
antiReferences.why: Name collision; US energy/probiotic drink, different product, not lifestyle-focused. Avoid any visual or copy similarities.

antiReferences.name: Aggressive energy drinks (e.g. Monster, Red Bull)
antiReferences.why: High-caffeine, sport/event-driven, masculine and extreme; clashes with wellness and everyday lifestyle cues.

brandFitRules: Accept brands that focus on lifestyle, wellness, and daily enjoyment rather than performance or sports.
Visuals must use light, pastel, or friendly colors. Avoid dark, metallic, harsh contrasts.
Copy should promote enjoyment, wellness, and sociability not discipline, optimization, or gym routines.
Exclude brands rooted in protein for muscle gain, energy drinks, or dietary restriction cultures.
Photography should show real-life, sunlit, relaxed or aspirational moments—not athletic, clinical or party scenes.
Reject any brand, design, or copy that resembles drinkpep.com in name, logo, or energy/functional focus.
